title: chenopodium album subsp. virgatum
intro: Chenopodium album subsp. virgatum, also known as the fat hen, is a subspecies of the common lambsquarters plant, a member of the Amaranthaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is said to have potential in treating conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Chenopodium album subsp. virgatum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and aid in digestion, as well as its potential to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
